highlights.item: Payload REST API routes are now mounted from the app root so public forms and auth calls stop failing with 404s.
The homepage instant quote form, the custom contact page, and the schedule page now all submit through first-party React Hook Form + Zod routes instead of relying on embedded CRM forms.
Lead submissions now write CRM sync metadata during the create path instead of relying on a broken post-create patch against form submissions.

The CRM sync path is now provider-abstracted: EngageBay remains the default provider, HubSpot can be configured as a fallback, and the active provider can be switched from the admin ops dashboard without schema changes.
Current EngageBay handoff is still returning `crmSyncStatus=failed_contact`, so Payload capture is reliable but EngageBay delivery is not yet production-ready.

next.item: Resolve EngageBay API quota/key-scope issues before treating CRM sync as production-ready.
If HubSpot is configured, verify provider switching in ops and use that path as the fallback before building quote-to-deal sync in phase `05-04`.
Optional next pass: replace remaining layout-builder contact blocks with the same first-party form UI used on `/contact`.
